articles.title: Heatwave warning issued for three states, 13 other areas under warning level
articles.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The state of Perlis, along with four districts in Kedah and one in Perak, are at heatwave level 2, while 13 other areas in Peninsular Malaysia are at level 1. Read full story

articles.title: House destroyed in afternoon blaze, no casualties reported
articles.summary: PETALING JAYA: A single-storey house was almost completely destroyed in a fire at Batu 8, Sijangkang, near Telok Panglima Garang, Kuala Langat, on Friday (March 27). Read full story

articles.title: CCTV captures woman dumping newborn in rubbish bin, probe underway
articles.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have obtained CCTV footage showing an unidentified woman allegedly abandoning a newborn baby girl in a rubbish bin outside a house in Taman Putra Ampang. Read full story

articles.title: Police investigating alleged extortion by Wangsa Maju resident reps
articles.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have received three reports over a viral video alleging that a member of the Federal Territories Residents' Representative Council (MPP) solicited protection money from a massage

articles.title: Penang govt mulls WFH arrangements, awaiting federal instructions
articles.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Penang state government is studying the implementation of working from home (WFH) for civil servants following challenges arising from rising fuel prices, particularly diesel, amid th

articles.title: Armed Forces deploy to borders against fuel subsidy misuse
articles.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) are deploying assets and resources to the borders to combat the diversion and leakage of fuel subsidies. Read full story

articles.title: Warisan reps want MACC to give updates on Sabah mining scandal probe
articles.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Parti Warisan assemblymen have sent a memorandum to the Malaysia Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) here to demand an update on the mining scandal allegedly involving state leaders. Read

articles.title: Diesel price cap in Sabah, Sarawak eases burden on rural communities
articles.summary: KUCHING: The government's decision to maintain diesel prices in Sabah and Sarawak has been welcomed by rural and inland communities who rely heavily on the fuel for their livelihoods. Read full story
